## New Hope on the Horizon – Horizon Zero Dawn

Nothing sets the gaming community astir quite like a new IP, especially one from a well-respected developer like Guerrilla Games. That’s right ladies and gentlemen, Horizon Zero Dawn – set on a post-apocalyptic Earth, teeming with robot dinosaurs – is finally here and it looks absolutely fantastic.

The game takes the form of a PlayStation 4 exclusive, third-person action RPG, centred around “Aloy, a hunter and archer living in a world overrun by robots. Having been cloistered her whole life, she sets out to discover the dangers that kept her sheltered.”

Firstly, it’s great to see big-name developers taking the initiative and creating more games with female protagonists, especially considering Aloy is not your stereotypical femme fatal. The character development and overall story shows a significant amount of thought and depth which leads to a captivating production that should keep most people interested throughout the game. 

Horizon Zero Dawn seems to follow the general formula (with a couple of quirks) for role-playing games at the current time with players being able to choose from ranged or melee weapons. There is, of course, a skill tree to facilitate gameplay improvements and provide the player with various goals to work towards. The skill tree also allows the game to cater to a wider variety of play styles (from ‘run and gun’ to stealth), based on which skills are developed.

The game is set in an open-world which leads to the expectation of many hours of playtime. Guerrilla Games does not seem to have disappointed too much in this category with the average reported play time coming in at around 40 hours. Most of this is filled with plenty of action and intrigue but there are some dull moments that require a bit of perseverance such as certain fetch quests (quite possibly the bane of my existence).

However, the graphics seem to be the real draw card in this game. The PS4 Pro version presents the player with lush, realistic landscapes that force you to just stop and stare… until you’re ravaged by a wandering dinosaur that is. 

As with any new game these days, HZD carries a hefty price tag. However, this purchase looks to be well worth it with reviews coming in at 8 to 9 out of 10. 
 
Horizon Zero Dawn is a beautiful open-world RPG which places the player on a quest to discover a plethora of dangers in a post-apocalyptic world. It has some serious, fun gameplay and stunning visuals which, along with the many positive reviews, leads me to encourage any PS4 owners to give this game a try. Enjoy the view!
